description_long: This object is a classic lever-action shotgun with a long, blued steel barrel and a wooden stock and fore-end. The stock is full-sized, made of polished wood with a smooth finish and a slight curve for shoulder support. The receiver and barrel are metallic, likely steel, with a dark, matte finish, and the lever loop is prominent beneath the receiver. The trigger guard is rounded and integrated with the lever, forming a continuous loop. The barrel is relatively slender and unadorned, with no visible ribbing or venting, and the overall design is streamlined and traditional. The shotgun's construction suggests durability and a focus on functional, utilitarian aesthetics.
description: A traditional lever-action shotgun with a long, blued steel barrel and polished wooden stock, featuring a rounded trigger guard integrated with the lever.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the shotgun, highlighting the wooden stock, metallic receiver, and long barrel.
description_view_1: Narrow end-on view showing the thin profile of the barrel and stock, with minimal visible detail.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, displaying the full length and details of the stock, receiver, and barrel.
description_view_3: End-on view from the stock, showing the slim profile and alignment of the barrel and stock.
attribute_barrel_configuration: single-barrel, no visible ribbing or venting, long barrel
attribute_trigger_guard_design: rounded, elongated, integrated with the lever, medium size, no finger grooves or reinforcement
